title: EMG recordings
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1ccie","text":"This is the standard protocol for recordings of bulk EMG used for Rodrigues-Vaz and Athalye et al, 2025.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"59h2","text":"-Mice were implanted with EMG electrodes in forelimb muscles, head plate, and GRIN lens and habituated to being head fixed.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bqlfv","text":"-Behavior setup was prepared and placed under microscope for in vivo experiments.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":61,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a9504","text":"-2-photon microscope was also prepared for simultaneous recordings. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"3g40u","text":"-Wear appropriate PPE as required by your institution. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"fn4k1","text":"•\tZIF-Clip adapter from Tucker-Davis Technologies (TDT) ZC16 headstage (16 channel ZIF-Clip)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7a86m","text":"•\tTDT RZ5D Bioamplifier processor (acquisition and synchronization device)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"20c1u","text":"•\tTDT PZ5-32 (Z-series 32-channel Neuro-digitizing preamplifier)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4jbp9","text":"•\tSynapse software from TDT for recording EMG","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Confirm EMG recordings are set to “true differential” in Synapse recording software.
Unplug headstage and return mouse to homecage.
At the end of the session, turn off all behavior boards, the imaging recording and lastly the EMG recordings.
Record session, confirming that all TTLs are being recorded as expected by design.
Optimize grounding if noise is not within normal range – mouse is grounded to air table.
Confirm that signals are recording when mouse moves.
Head fix mouse in behavior setup under microscope and connect ZC16 headstage to implanted connector.
Store all cables in safe location
creator: Ines Rodrigues-Vaz
